purpose: Wave-column layout — the primary visualization for parallel swarms. Renders one column per wave, each with a WaveHeader (wave id+name, completed/total count, StatusBadge) and the agents in that wave as AgentCards. An absolutely-positioned SVG overlay (chain-svg) draws dependency lines between cards via drawDependencyLines/drawSiblingLines from utils/dependencyLines.js; setupCardHoverEffects wires hover-highlight. A ResizeObserver redraws the lines whenever the container resizes (layout shift moves cards). Surfaces an unblocked-tasks toast for 8s via appState.unblockedTasks + auto-dismiss timer. Supports activeStatFilter to drop non-matching waves.

exports.name: WavePipeline
exports.signature: { status: { agents[], waves[], chains[] }, activeStatFilter: string|null, onAgentClick: (agent) => void, progressData?: object }

gotchas: Dependency lines depend on the DOM `data-agent-id` attribute on AgentCard — renaming or removing that attribute silently breaks the SVG overlay.

ResizeObserver re-runs drawDependencyLines/drawSiblingLines on every container resize; for very wide swarms with many edges this can be expensive. The lines util has its own internal cache keyed on card geometry.
Wave column ordering depends on the order of `waves` in initialization.json — the master is responsible for ordering correctly; this component does not sort.
When activeStatFilter is set, entire waves disappear if no agent matches — the column gap can collapse, causing dependency lines to redraw to surprising positions.
Unblocked tasks toast auto-dismiss timer (8s) restarts on every appState.unblockedTasks change — repeatedly unblocking new tasks resets the timer rather than queueing toasts.
patterns: Two parallel maps built per render: agentMap (id→agent) and per-wave aggregates (completedByWave, totalByWave) for the header badge.
drawDependencyLines is invoked both inside the main effect AND inside the ResizeObserver callback to handle layout shifts after initial draw.
Imperative DOM querying for cards (`querySelectorAll('.agent-card[data-agent-id]')`) instead of refs — necessary because cards are rendered dynamically.
conventions: Wave column class chain: wave-column (+wave-active / +wave-done) mirrors wave-header modifiers.
Wave header shows count badge only when totalCount > 0.
